awareness.title: Stage 1: Awareness
awareness.headline: Get Found by the Right Buyers
awareness.seo_title: Awareness Stage — Get Found by the Right Buyers | Richard Norwood, PMP
awareness.seo_description: Most buyers do extensive research before they ever talk to a salesperson. If your business isn't visible where they're looking, you don't exist. Learn how to fix your awareness stage.
awareness.og_description: Your buyers are researching right now. Are they finding you — or your competitors?
awareness.problem_hook: Your team is great at what they do. But the right buyers can't find you.
symptoms: Leads come from referrals — and only referrals
Your website gets traffic but not from your ideal buyer
Competitors with weaker offerings outrank you in search
Your LinkedIn presence generates likes, not conversations
You've invested in content but can't connect it to pipeline
awareness.solution_summary: Awareness isn't about volume. It's about being visible to the right people, in the right places, with the right message — before a conversation ever starts. We help you build a positioning strategy that puts your business where buyers are already looking and gives them a reason to pay attention.

stat_callout.label: of B2B buyers complete their research before talking to sales.

fabs.advantage: Places your business where buyers are already looking — search, social, and referral surfaces.
fabs.benefit: You become visible and relevant before a conversation ever starts.

fabs.advantage: Helps buyers name the problem with practical, accessible insights.
fabs.benefit: Builds early trust so your business is the first point of reference.

fabs.advantage: Routes early attention from offline and online touchpoints to a meaningful next step.

consideration.title: Stage 2: Consideration
consideration.headline: Make It Easy to Evaluate You
consideration.seo_title: Consideration Stage — Make It Easy to Evaluate You | Richard Norwood, PMP
consideration.seo_description: Your website looks fine, but it's not helping buyers decide. They're comparing you against two or three alternatives right now. Here's how to win the evaluation.
consideration.og_description: Your buyers are comparing you right now. Is your website helping them decide — or sending them to a competitor?
consideration.problem_hook: Buyers visit your site, browse for a minute, and leave. They're evaluating — and you're not making it easy.
symptoms: Prospects say 'we went with someone else' without explaining why
Your website describes what you do but not why it matters
You rely on sales calls to explain what the website should have already communicated
There's no structured path from 'interested' to 'let's talk'
You have no way to stay present during a long evaluation cycle
consideration.solution_summary: Consideration is where buyers decide whether you're worth a deeper look. Your website needs to act like a guide — not a brochure. We help you build evaluation-ready structures that show proof, teach how to think about the problem, and make it obvious why your approach is the right fit.

stat_callout.label: of the buyer journey is now completed digitally, without talking to sales.

fabs.advantage: Provides proof, context, and examples that help buyers understand fit — not just browse.
fabs.benefit: Your website becomes a decision-support tool, not a digital brochure.

fabs.advantage: Continues the conversation with relevant, helpful messages — no pressure.
fabs.benefit: Keeps you present during complex evaluation without forcing a sales call.

fabs.advantage: Validates your expertise with proof aligned to the buyer's actual concerns.
fabs.benefit: Reduces uncertainty and builds authority early.

decision.title: Stage 3: Decision
decision.headline: Remove the Risk
decision.seo_title: Decision Stage — Remove the Risk That Stalls Deals | Richard Norwood, PMP
decision.seo_description: Deals stall when buyers can't resolve their concerns on their own. Unclear pricing, missing proof, and complicated next steps cost you revenue every month.
decision.og_description: Deals don't die because of competition. They die because buyers can't resolve their concerns.
decision.problem_hook: Proposals go out. Then silence. The deal doesn't die — it just stops moving.
symptoms: Proposals sit untouched for weeks
You win on relationship but lose when buyers compare on paper
Prospects ghost after what felt like a strong meeting
Your close rate depends on who's selling, not the system
Buyers can't find answers to basic questions without calling you
decision.solution_summary: Decision is about risk removal, not persuasion. Buyers don't need more pressure — they need clarity. We replace static pages with transparent proof, practical FAQs, and simple next steps that resolve objections before they become blockers.

stat_callout.label: of deals in the pipeline are lost to 'no decision' — not to a competitor.

fabs.advantage: Provides trust signals, FAQs, and evidence relevant to the buyer's specific concerns.
fabs.benefit: Buyers can validate fit without unnecessary back-and-forth.

fabs.advantage: Handles concerns consistently — not just when a strong seller happens to be in the room.
fabs.benefit: Reduces decision risk through clarity, not pressure.

fabs.advantage: Makes it safe and straightforward for the right buyer to move forward.
fabs.benefit: Protects both sides from a poor fit.

conversion.title: Stage 4: Conversion
conversion.headline: Turn Commitment Into a Clean Start
conversion.seo_title: Conversion Stage — Turn Signed Deals Into Clean Starts | Richard Norwood, PMP
conversion.seo_description: The deal is signed. Now what? Clunky onboarding, unclear ownership, and lost context turn wins into churn risks. Here's how to protect the momentum.
conversion.og_description: A signed deal isn't a win. It's a promise. Here's how to keep it.
conversion.problem_hook: You celebrate the close — then scramble to figure out what happens next.
symptoms: Onboarding is different every time depending on who's running it
Context from the sales process gets lost in the handoff
New clients feel confused about what happens after they sign
Your team argues about who owns the relationship post-sale
First impressions as a vendor don't match the sales experience
conversion.solution_summary: Conversion is the center of the journey — where commitment becomes a relationship. We make sure the path from signature to kickoff is smooth, professional, and preserves every ounce of context from the sales process. No lost momentum. No confused handoffs.

stat_callout.label: new clients consider switching providers within the first 90 days — usually because of onboarding friction.

fabs.advantage: Simplifies the path from agreement to action so the commitment feels professional.
fabs.benefit: Eliminates unnecessary admin at the most important moment.

fabs.advantage: Defines who owns the relationship after the sale so nothing is lost.
fabs.benefit: Preserves momentum from pre-sale through onboarding.

fabs.advantage: Moves from agreement to action with clear steps and intact context.
fabs.benefit: A good decision becomes a good experience from day one.

retention.title: Stage 5: Retention
retention.headline: Protect and Grow the Relationship
retention.seo_title: Retention Stage — Protect and Grow the Relationship | Richard Norwood, PMP
retention.seo_description: Customer churn is a revenue problem hiding in plain sight. Most businesses treat retention as an afterthought. Here's why the right side of the journey matters more than the left.
retention.og_description: Your biggest growth lever isn't new logos. It's the customers you already have.
retention.problem_hook: You're so focused on new business that your best customers are quietly drifting away.
symptoms: Churn surprises you — you didn't see it coming
Renewals depend on the account manager's memory, not a system
Upsell attempts feel forced and damage trust
You don't know which accounts are thriving and which are at risk
Your best customers haven't referred anyone in over a year
retention.solution_summary: Retention isn't an afterthought — it's the other half of the revenue engine. We help you build visibility into customer health, surface risks before they become churn, and create expansion pathways rooted in real value. Because if the right side is weak, the left side gets more expensive every quarter.

stat_callout.label: more expensive to acquire a new customer than to retain an existing one.

fabs.advantage: Surfaces adoption gaps and drift before they become churn.
fabs.benefit: Protects the value you promised during the sale.

fabs.advantage: Creates structured plays for accounts that need attention or are approaching renewal.
Identifies growth opportunities rooted in real value, not forced upsells.
fabs.benefit: Turns satisfied customers into a reliable source of referrals and revenue.
